etymology.description: semantic 工 (gōng, "work") + phonetic 弋 (yì) -> form
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 工 (gōng, "work") contributes the idea of structured or crafted work, while the phonetic 弋 (yì) provides the sound. The combination suggests a crafted or established form, thus leading to the meaning "form."

examples.chinese: 他设计了一种新式的椅子(他設計了一種新式的椅子)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shèjìle yī zhǒng xīn shì de yǐzi.
examples.english: He designed a new form of chair.

examples.chinese: 这种建筑形式很受欢迎(這種建築形式很受歡迎)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng jiànzhù xíngshì hěn shòu huānyíng.
examples.english: This form of architecture is very popular.

examples.chinese: 考试采用了新的评分形式(考試採用了新的評分形式)。
examples.pinyin: Kǎoshì cǎiyòngle xīn de píngfēn xíngshì.
examples.english: The exam adopted a new form of grading.
